sampleQuestions.stem: Consider the following logical inferences. I_1: If it rains then the cricket match will not be played. The cricket match was played. Inference: There was no rain. I_2: If it rains then the cricket match will not be playe

Consider the following two statements. S_1: If a candidate is known to be corrupt, then he will not be elected. S_2: If a candidate is kind, he will be elected. Which one of the following statements follows from S_1 and

Show that the conclusion (r -> q) follows from the premises: p, (p -> q) v (p -> r), (r -> q). More precisely, given the premises p, (p -> q) v (p -> r), show that (r -> q) is a logical consequence.
Show that the conclusion (r -> q) follows from the premises: p, (p -> q) ∨ (p ∧ (r -> q)).
Define the validity of a well-formed formula (wff)?
